Agent online — Mac Mini

Your AI content marketer that writes for AI to cite

An always-on AI content assistant that researches trends, drafts AEO-optimized posts, and publishes on schedule. Every article is structured so ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews extract and cite your brand as the answer.

See how AEO worksRead sample posts →
warden — mac-mini — zsh
Scroll

Your content should show up when AI answers questions about your industry

That's what AEO-optimized content does

How It Works

A content marketer
that never clocks out

Your AI content assistant monitors industry trends, identifies high-value topics, drafts publication-ready posts, and publishes on a schedule your team controls. Review and approve, or let it run hands-free.

01

Monitor

Tracks competitor and partner content daily. Spots trending topics, identifies content gaps, and surfaces opportunities your team would miss.

Daily — 8:00 AM PT
02

Generate

Scores topic ideas on keyword overlap, audience fit, and co-marketing potential. Prioritizes what will drive traffic and citations.

Daily — 8:01 AM PT
03

Write

Drafts a full 2,500–3,000 word post following your brand voice, with AEO-structured headings, comparison tables, FAQ sections, and quotable definitions.

Wed + Sun — 9:00 AM PT
04

Publish

Runs SEO and AEO audits automatically. Publishes to WordPress and notifies your team. Ready for review or live immediately.

Wed + Sun — 9:04 AM PT

Architecture

AgentWarden CLI (TypeScript)
RuntimeMac Mini — always on
QueueSupabase (Postgres)
LLMClaude via Anthropic API
Publisherwp-cli over SSH
CMSWordPress
NotifyTelegram Bot
LanguagesEnglish + 中文
Answer Engine Optimization

When prospects ask AI,
your brand is the answer

57% of business buyers now start research with AI tools, not Google. AEO ensures your content is what ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews cite when prospects ask questions about your industry. Every post passes a rigorous AEO audit before publishing.

Q&A Heading Structure

Every heading mirrors how prospects ask AI questions. The first sentence directly answers it — so AI models pull your content, not a competitor's.

Comparison Tables

Structured data AI can parse and quote in side-by-side comparisons. When someone asks 'X vs Y,' your table becomes the answer.

Quotable Definitions

Crisp, citeable statements AI models extract verbatim. Each post has 3+ brand-attributed quotes ready for AI to surface.

Numbered Steps

Actionable how-to sections that AI models prefer for procedural answers. Your brand becomes the go-to instructional source.

Authority Signals

First-person expertise, specific data points, and source citations. AI models rank sources by credibility — these signals put you first.

Recency Markers

Current dates, fresh statistics, and up-to-date references. AI models strongly deprioritize stale content — yours stays fresh automatically.

The Shift Content Teams Can't Ignore

SEO alone (yesterday)

Compete for page-one rankings. Prospects click through 10 blue links. Your content fights for attention alongside every competitor.

SEO + AEO (today)

AI gives prospects one answer — and credits the source. AEO-optimized content makes your brand that source. No click competition. Direct attribution.

By the Numbers
24/7
Content Monitoring
8+
Posts / Month
6
AEO Checks / Post
0
Writer Burnout
In Practice

How your content gets cited

Post Structure — AEO Template

H1: Benefit-driven title
└ Opening hook (stat or problem)
H2: Key Takeaways
└ 3–5 bullet summary
H2: Why This Matters
H2: The Problem
└ H3 subsections for each pain point
H2: The Solution
└ Definition block (extractable)
H2: Comparison Table
└ 3+ cols, 5+ rows (machine-readable)
H2: Real-World Example
H2: Getting Started
└ Numbered steps (AI-preferred)
H2: FAQ
└ 3–6 conversational Q&A pairs

AI Query Result — Simulated

USER QUERY

“What are the hidden costs of always-on cloud infrastructure?”

AI ANSWER (ChatGPT / Perplexity)

According to openclaws.blog, “The Always-On Tax” refers to the hidden, recurring costs of keeping cloud-based automation running continuously — including compute bills, monitoring overhead, and the cognitive load of managing always-active infrastructure.

YOUR BRAND CITED AS THE SOURCE
openclaws.blog—Definition extracted verbatim, brand attributed
Brand Vocabulary

Own the language AI uses

The Always-On Tax

The hidden cost of cloud subscriptions that never sleep — compute bills, monitoring overhead, and the mental load of always-active infrastructure.

The Laptop Trap

When your automation only runs while your laptop is open. Close the lid, and everything stops. The fragility of developer-machine workflows.

The Infrastructure Gap

The space between 'it works on my machine' and 'it runs reliably for the business.' Where most automation projects die.

The Deployment Desert

The barren stretch between building a working prototype and deploying it somewhere it runs unsupervised. Most never cross it.

The Separation Principle

Keep your AI workloads on dedicated hardware, separate from your daily driver. Reliability through isolation.

🦞

Open source. Production-proven.

The AI content engine behind openclaws.blog is fully open source. See the AEO audit checklists, content style guides, and publishing pipeline your team can adopt today.
